transcript: And he's down there in lane 7, an unusual position for Phelps, second from the bottom. Right down there near the bottom, it's all about the finishing speed, and you said it Dan, he just needs to find his legs, the last 50, that's what he says, that's so much, but he had that double last night coming off the 200 IM, but there's no doubt he will not be first going into this one. Tim Phillips looks good in lane five. Stubblefield has the lead, and Phelps was in fourth. Looking pretty good there. He actually was not bad going out. He's running for it flat. I thought he might be a little slower. You know Shields is going to put together a good one. He's right about that. But look at this line. Oh my goodness. Here we go again. Thurps is kicking it in in lane seven. And he might win it. He did it. He does. Shields is second. Well, you just said you're never going to stop doubting him now. As once again, Phelps proves when it's all on the line, he's got something special. He will indeed swim for his fourth Olympic gold medal in this one, like the 200 IM. No swimmer from the United States has ever won four in a row.
